entities.name: UPLAND RICE
entities.description: A type of rice grown in non-flooded areas used in intercropping studies.

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop evaluated for its canopy structure and phenology in intercropping.

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: A medium deep soil type used as the growth medium for the intercrop components.

entities.name: CROP PERFORMANCE RATIO
entities.description: A metric used to determine the productivity of intercrop components relative to sole crops.

entities.name: PHENOLOGY
entities.description: The timing of plant development stages, used to distinguish pigeonpea cultivars.

entities.name: CANOPY STRUCTURE
entities.description: The physical architecture of the plant canopy, which influences light transmission.

entities.name: LIGHT TRANSMISSION COEFFICIENTS
entities.description: A measurement (K) indicating the amount of light passing through the crop canopy.

entities.name: RELATIVE HEIGHT
entities.description: The height of intercropped plants relative to each other, affecting competition.

entities.name: COMPETITIVE ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of a crop to compete for resources, such as light, in an intercropping system.

entities.name: LOW LIGHT AND SHADING
entities.description: Environmental conditions resulting from canopy overlap that can negatively affect crop yield.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE PHASE
entities.description: The growth stage of the plant where it is most sensitive to light and shading.

relationships.description: Intercropped together to examine productivity and interactions
